originalTitle: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
title: Our Drawing Hero is FREE! Biniam Solomon is Home!
content:
Super Happy News from Africa!

Hello, amazing friends! Your kid-friendly news reporter is here with some SUPER-DUPER exciting news from our big, beautiful continent of Africa! Get ready to clap your hands and cheer, because someone very special is finally free and back home. His name is Biniam Solomon, and he is a wonderful artist! Can you imagine being away from your family, your friends, and your home for a very, very long time? Well, Biniam Solomon was. He spent fifteen whole years away. That's like from when you were a tiny baby, until now, and then almost twice that time again! But guess what? He is now free! Woohoo!

Who is Biniam Solomon?

Biniam Solomon is a hero who loves to draw. He is what we call a 'cartoonist'. Have you ever seen funny pictures in books, newspapers, or on TV that tell a story or make you laugh? Those are cartoons! Biniam was very, very good at making these special drawings. He lived in a country called Eritrea. Eritrea is a beautiful country right here in Africa, with amazing beaches and tall mountains. Biniam's drawings were not just funny; they were also very clever. They made people think about important things and sometimes showed ways that things could be better. Did you know? Cartoons are like secret messages sometimes! Artists use them to share their ideas and feelings with lots of people, even without using many words!

What are Cartoons?

Imagine you have a great idea or something funny to say. You could write it down, right? Or you could draw it! That's what Biniam did. He drew pictures that were smart and sometimes showed what he thought about the world around him. His drawings were special because they were both funny and made people really think. He was known for his 'witty' and 'critical' cartoons. This means his drawings were very clever and made people think about important things, sometimes even showing how things could be improved. Before a time when people in Eritrea couldn't share their ideas freely, Biniam's cartoons were loved by many. People looked forward to seeing his new drawings. They would smile, laugh, and then think about the messages in his art.

A Long, Long Time Away

One day, Biniam Solomon was taken away. He was put in a place called prison. Prison is usually a place where people go if they have broken the rules or done something wrong. But the very sad thing is, nobody ever told Biniam what rule he had broken. He was kept in prison for fifteen years without anyone telling him *why* he was there. This is called being held 'without charge'. It means no one ever said what he did wrong. Can you imagine how confusing and unfair that would be? It was a very long time for him to be away from his family, his pencils, and his drawing paper. Fifteen years is longer than many of your teachers have been teaching in your school! It's a huge part of a person's life.

Eritrea: A Home in Africa

Eritrea is a country on the east side of Africa. It has a long coast next to the Red Sea, which is full of amazing fish and sea creatures. It's a land of history and beautiful landscapes. Biniam is from this special place. Imagine being away from your own country, your own home, for such a long time. It must have been very hard. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world, and it has 54 countries! Eritrea is one of them, with its own special culture and traditions.

What Does 'Free Press' Mean?

Before Biniam was taken away, there was something called a 'free press' in Eritrea. This means that people were allowed to write, draw, and share their ideas without anyone stopping them or being afraid. It's like being able to speak freely in your classroom or draw any picture you want without worrying. A 'free press' helps everyone learn new things and helps a country grow stronger because all ideas can be shared. But then, the 'free press was closed'. This means people could not share their thoughts or drawings as freely anymore. It was a sad time for artists and writers like Biniam.

Freedom at Last!

After all those years, Biniam Solomon is finally free! This is such wonderful news. It means he can go home, see his family, hug his loved ones, and maybe even pick up his pencils and draw again. Freedom is a precious gift. It means you can choose where to go, what to do, and who to be with. It means you can share your ideas and your art with the world. Think about how happy his family must be! It's like finding something you thought was lost forever, but even better, because it's a person! It's a celebration for everyone who believes in fairness and freedom.

What Can We Learn?

This story teaches us a few important things, my young friends. First, drawings and art are powerful! They can make people think, laugh, and even dream. Second, it's really important for people to be fair to each other. Everyone deserves to know why they are being treated a certain way. And third, freedom is a very special thing that we should all treasure. Being able to share our ideas and live our lives happily is so important. So, let's all send a big cheer to Biniam Solomon! Welcome home, Biniam! We hope you pick up your pencils soon and share your amazing art with the world again! Hip hip hooray for freedom and for our drawing hero!
summary: Biniam Solomon, a talented cartoonist from Eritrea, has been set free after spending 15 years in prison without ever being told why, bringing great joy and a lesson about fairness and freedom.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone should be treated fairly and know why they are being held, and it reminds us that freedom to share ideas, like through drawing, is a precious gift for everyone.

title: Chinese national arrested over attempt to smuggle 2,000 queen ants from Kenya
content: The suspect had packed some ants in test tubes while others were concealed in tissue paper rolls, prosecutor says.

originalTitle: Iran’s Mojtaba Khamenei issues first statement as Supreme Leader amid war
title: News from Far Away! Iran's New Important Leader Speaks!
content:
Hello, amazing kids! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here, ready to share some interesting news from far, far away! Today, we're going on an imaginary journey to a country called Iran. It's a big place on another continent, just like how Africa is a big continent. We're going to learn about a very important person who just got a very big job there.

A New Leader for Iran!

Imagine your school has a head teacher. That person is very important, right? They make big decisions for the whole school. They help everyone learn and grow. Well, in Iran, there is a very special leader. His name is Mr. Mojtaba Khamenei. It's a big name, so we can just call him Mr. Mojtaba! He just got a very important new job. He is now the top leader for all the people in Iran. He is like the main boss, like a super head teacher for the whole country. This is a very big moment for Iran. When someone new gets such an important job, everyone wants to know what they will say first. Mr. Mojtaba stood up and talked to many people. He shared his first big message since becoming the new important leader. People all over the country listened very carefully to his words. They wanted to know about his plans for their country. Did you know? When a new leader starts, it's often a time for new ideas and plans for the country's future!

What is a Supreme Leader?

Mr. Mojtaba is called the 'Supreme Leader' of Iran. The word 'Supreme' means the highest or the most important. So, he is the most important leader in the whole country. He has a very special role. He helps guide all the people in Iran. He makes very big decisions that affect everyone's lives. These decisions can be about how people live, how they learn, and how they stay safe. It's a huge responsibility! Think about your parents or your guardians. They make important decisions for your family to keep you safe and happy. A Supreme Leader does that, but for millions and millions of people! He has to think about what is best for the country every single day. He has to think about peace and how people can live good lives. He makes choices that can shape the future of his country. Did you know? Different countries have different kinds of leaders! Some have presidents, some have prime ministers, and some have kings or queens. They all have important jobs to lead their people.

Where is Iran?

Let's go on a quick imaginary journey! Iran is a big country far, far away from us here in Africa. If you look at a map of the world, you would find Iran in a big continent called Asia. It's east of some parts of Africa, across a big ocean or some big seas. It's a place with a very long history, much older than many countries. Imagine buildings that are thousands of years old! Iran has big mountains, some deserts, and beautiful green places too. It's a country with many different kinds of people and cultures. Imagine yummy food, colorful carpets, and beautiful stories passed down through generations. People in Iran speak a language called Farsi. It's a very different language from the ones we speak here. It's always fun to learn about places far away, isn't it? Did you know? Iran is famous for its beautiful poetry and ancient Persian gardens! Many people also love their special tea and delicious sweet pastries.

What Did He Say?

So, what did this new important leader, Mr. Mojtaba, say in his first big talk? He spoke very strongly and bravely. He said that Iran would stay strong. He said they would keep working very hard for their country. Sometimes, countries face big challenges or problems, just like sometimes friends might have a disagreement. Mr. Mojtaba said that Iran would continue to be brave and work to solve its big problems. He wants Iran to be strong and protect its people. His message was about not giving up and always standing up for what they believe is right for their country. He wants to make sure Iran is safe and that its people can continue to live their lives. Leaders often talk about making their country strong and protecting its borders and people. Did you know? Even when things are difficult, talking about problems and trying to find solutions is always the best way forward!

Why Is This Important?

Why does it matter what Mr. Mojtaba said? Well, when a leader speaks, especially a very important one, their words are like big plans or big promises. What leaders say can give people hope. It can also help everyone understand what the future might look like for their country. His words can help shape how Iran works with other countries around the world too. When leaders speak strongly about being brave and protecting their country, it shows they care deeply about their people. We hope that all leaders in all countries always talk about peace, kindness, and working together. We hope they always try to make sure their people are safe and happy. Every child, no matter where they live, deserves to grow up in a peaceful world. That's why leaders' words are so important. They can help build a good future for everyone. Did you know? Thinking about others and helping each other is a way we can all be leaders for peace in our own lives, even as kids!

Thinking About Peace

Even though Mr. Mojtaba spoke about Iran being strong and solving problems, we always hope that leaders everywhere are working for peace. What is peace? Peace means no fighting. It means people being kind to each other. It means helping friends, sharing toys, and listening to each other. For countries, it means working together and solving problems with words, not with fights. Every time leaders talk, we hope they remember how important peace is. We hope they choose ways to make sure everyone can live safely and happily. Just like in your classroom, if everyone works together and is kind, your classroom is a peaceful place. The same is true for countries. We can all hope for leaders to choose peace and kindness always. This makes the world a better place for all children, everywhere! Let's all keep hoping for a world full of peace and friendship. Thank you for listening, amazing kids!
